title: Antibody Conjugations via Glycosyl Remodeling
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1ekk0","text":"The antibody Fc-glycans are interesting targets for conjugation of cytotoxic compounds due to their localization and their chemical composition. In striving to obtain site-specific conjugates, the antibody Fc-glycans have been explored in numerous ways. Here we present a two-step enzymatic methodology coupled to click-chemistry for conjugation at the core GlcNAc of the Fc-glycan resulting in ADCs that are homogenous with a DAR 2.0, retain antigen binding, and display cytotoxic anti-tumor effects both in vitro and in vivo.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"qfqjx","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
creator: Protocols Importer
